classification_rationale: Vendor operates a customer-facing AI ticket-triage system that processes customer-supplied message content (including incidental PII) but routes only to human agents who handle all customer-facing communication. The AI does not respond to customers directly and operates under human-confirmed autonomy. Combined with current SOC 2 Type II and ISO 27001 attestations, a signed DPA, and disclosed sub-processor inventory, the use case fits Tier 2 moderate. Conditional approval is warranted because the model receives PII in real time and the 90-day transcript retention requires periodic verification of stated controls.

evidence_cited.reasoning: Reported as 'customer_facing', which under the risk taxonomy places the vendor in the Tier 2-3 band. The 'human_confirmed' autonomy on all features pulls the classification toward Tier 2 rather than Tier 3.
Ticket triage is 'human_confirmed': the AI routes to a human agent queue, but the human agent makes the customer-facing decision. The human-in-the-loop precondition holds.
Suggested reply drafts are reviewed and edited by human agents before sending. The model does not autonomously communicate with customers.

Customer name, contact information, and incidental PII in message content are processed in real time. Vendor states no model fine-tuning on customer content. The PII flow elevates the tier from Tier 1 productivity to Tier 2 moderate.
Vendor states 90-day transcript retention and PII masking on account numbers. The 90-day window introduces a re-review trigger; transcript handling controls warrant verification.
Anthropic disclosed as sub-processor for model inference, with stated no-training-use commitment in DPA. The disclosure satisfies the model-supply-chain transparency expectation for Tier 2.
SOC 2 Type II (2025-11) and ISO 27001 (2025-06) both current. Both attestations are expected at this tier; absence of either would force escalation.

required_mitigations: Verify the no-training-use commitment against the actual Anthropic DPA at contract renewal.
Quarterly review of transcript-retention controls (90-day purge) and PII-masking efficacy on account numbers.
Monitor for scope expansion from human-confirmed reply drafts toward customer-facing autonomous response, which would re-trigger Tier 3 review.
Confirm Canadian-region data pinning is actually applied for in-scope tenants (vendor states the option exists; deploying-organization configuration must enable it).
